mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the risk-free discount rate applied to equity cash flows, directly boosting S&P 500 valuations over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show no statistically significant positive correlation with FEDFUNDS declines over a 3-year rolling window (Rank IC < 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate rises above 4.0% within a single month, it signals a rapid deterioration in labor demand that precedes corporate earnings contractions, depressing equity returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns remain positive or neutral in more than 50% of instances where UNRATE breaches the 4.0% threshold within a month.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous declines in real personal spending and industrial production indicate a synchronized demand-supply shock that compresses corporate margins and equity valuations, predicting negative forward returns.
failure_condition: The joint decline signal fails to produce negative 20-day forward returns in more than 40% of observed instances over a 5-year backtest.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of tight housing market conditions, construction-related inflation persists, forcing the Fed to maintain restrictive policy, which suppresses equity multiples over a 1-month horizon.
regime_hint: housing supply constrained environment with sticky inflation
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward returns are not significantly lower when HOUST remains below 1.2 million units compared to periods above this threshold (t-test p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: When the CPI index exceeds 305.0, sticky inflation erodes real yields and triggers valuation compression, leading to negative equity returns over the next week.
failure_condition: Weekly forward returns do not exhibit a statistically significant negative skew when CPIAUCSL trades above 305.0 across multiple inflationary episodes.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained month-over-month growth in total nonfarm payrolls reflects robust economic activity that supports corporate revenue growth, generating positive S&P 500 returns over a 1-month horizon.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show no positive correlation with PAYEMS growth rates over a rolling 3-year window (Rank IC < 0.04).

mechanism_narrative: The combination of rising unemployment and falling industrial production captures a wage-price spiral breakdown that historically precedes equity drawdowns, predicting negative returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict negative 60-day forward returns in more than 45% of historical instances over a 10-year walk-forward test.

mechanism_narrative: When new housing starts fall below 1.0 million units annually, it signals a sharp contraction in business investment and consumer confidence, depressing S&P 500 returns over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns remain positive in more than 50% of cases where HOUST breaches the 1000.0 threshold, invalidating the predictive power of housing starts.
